description.current: Validate layouts across 8 device viewports (375px–1920px): layout, touch targets, typography, content parity, orientation, overflow. Use after responsive redesigns, CSS refactors, mobile launches.
issues: Description starts with tool (8 viewports) before explaining the WHY. Pushy on dimensions without motivation.
Missing context: 'responsive redesigns' assumes user knows what to validate FOR. Better: 'ensure mobile usability after redesign' or 'validate breakpoint behavior'

issues.description: Device matrix lacks Safari/iOS specific behavior. Table includes 'iPhone SE/15/15 Pro Max' but no guidance on WebKit rendering differences, safe area insets, notch handling, or bouncy overscroll. Mobile Safari != Chrome DevTools emulation.
Phase 2 section structure uses 'Phase 2a/2b/2c/2d/2e' nested phases which is confusing. Reads like 5 separate validation runs instead of 5 checks per viewport iteration.

Touch target validation (Line 85-91) claims 44x44 CSS pixels is the standard but doesn't cite source (WCAG? Apple HIG? Android?). Standard varies: WCAG 2.5.5 requires 44x44 CSS pixels but only at C level; iOS recommends 44x44pt (88px at 2x DPR). No guidance on which to use.

Typography validation (Line 93-99) recommends '16px on mobile prevents iOS zoom' - this is outdated. Modern iOS (14+) doesn't auto-zoom on 16px+ input fields. Guidance should note iOS version or remove the claim.

Evidence structure section (Lines 138-155) specifies per-viewport directories (mobile-375/, mobile-393/) but viewport naming doesn't match table header names (iPhone SE vs mobile-375). Inconsistency in naming convention.
Verdict rules (Lines 157-167) define 'CONDITIONAL' for 'minor issues' but doesn't quantify. Is a single layout shift CONDITIONAL or FAIL? Ambiguous threshold.

Phase 4 orientation testing uses a single ratio (375x667 ↔ 667x375) but doesn't address cross-orientation consistency. What happens at iPad orientation change? Device rotation physics vary. No guidance on when to test orientation.

strengths: Device matrix is comprehensive and practical - 8 viewports cover realistic device range
Per-viewport checklists (layout, touch, typography, content parity) are concrete and testable
Orientation testing explicitly covers portrait/landscape - often forgotten
Cross-breakpoint analysis section catches intermediate breakpoints (769px) - good edge case thinking
Evidence directory structure is systematic and audit-friendly

bundled_resources.missing_scripts_opportunity: No bundled Playwright/Chrome DevTools MCP wrappers. Skill references 'browser_resize(width, height)' and 'emulate(viewport=...)' but doesn't show actual tool syntax. Could ship example calls for both Playwright and Chrome DevTools MCPs.

top_fixes: Add iOS-specific viewport notes: WebKit rendering, safe areas, notch handling, bouncy scroll behavior
Cite touch target standard sources: clarify WCAG C (44x44 CSS px) vs Apple HIG (44x44pt) vs Android Material (48dp minimum)
Update iOS font zoom claim to note iOS 14+ behavior change
Rename evidence directories from pixel widths to device names for consistency with device matrix headers
Define 'CONDITIONAL' verdict threshold: e.g., 'single cosmetic layout shift' vs 'affects primary content area'
Add orientation testing guidance: when to test rotation (tablets always, phones context-dependent), account for landscape keyboard on iPad

test_prompts.prompt: Just redesigned our e-commerce checkout for mobile-first. Desktop version is narrower now. Need to validate layouts don't break between 375px phones and 1920px monitors. Where do I start?
test_prompts.expected_output: Skill directs 8-viewport matrix validation, emphasizes content parity (critical for checkout), touch target checks on mobile. Captures screenshots at 375/393/430 (mobile), 768/1024 (tablet), 1280/1440/1920 (desktop). Verdict checklist ensures no horizontal overflow at any size. Evidence structured per viewport with layout/touch/typography findings.

test_prompts.prompt: Users report layout 'shifts' on iPad when rotating between portrait and landscape. Sometimes text disappears. How do I systematically test orientation changes?
test_prompts.expected_output: Skill covers Phase 4 orientation testing: portrait and landscape captures at iPad viewports (768x1024 and 1024x768). Checks content reflow, navigation state consistency, no critical content cut off. Highlights safe area inset handling for notch-equipped devices. Produces evidence comparing before/after rotation.

test_prompts.prompt: Touch targets on mobile buttons too small - users can't tap. What size should buttons be? How do I verify across all phones?
test_prompts.expected_output: Skill references 44x44 CSS pixel minimum (with note on WCAG/HIG standards). Phase 2c directs per-viewport touch target audit: measure button dimensions in screenshots, verify ≥8px spacing between targets. Tests on all 3 mobile viewports (375/393/430). Clear FAIL verdict if any button <44x44 or spacing <8px.
